what_it_does: A tiny global-window React component rendering a floating bottom-right 'Chats' pill — a speech-bubble SVG icon, the word 'Chats', and an unread count badge — for the platform dashboard.
observations: Single function Chats({count = 21}) defaulting the badge to 21
Two overlapping speech-bubble SVG paths (one transformed/translated) form a layered chat glyph
Markup carries role="button" but no onClick handler
Class cv-chats and child .count are styled in platform.css (fixed position, pill shape, red count badge)
conventions: Global-window component registration matching the kit's Babel-in-browser pattern
Presentation delegated to cv-* classes in platform.css; no inline styles
concerns: role="button" with no keyboard handler or onClick — non-functional affordance
Default count of 21 is a hardcoded placeholder value
notes: If briefing someone: this is the static floating Chats pill in the dashboard's corner — purely visual, count is a prop with a placeholder default.
questions: Is the Chats pill meant to open a panel/route when clicked, or is it decorative in this kit?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header says 'floating bottom-right pill' and that is exactly what it renders.
apparent_themes: persistent corner affordance with unread badge
intention_signals: Aims to depict a persistent chat/messaging entry point with an unread indicator in the platform dashboard corner.
contribution: Contributes the floating Chats-pill component (icon + label + unread count badge) for the platform dashboard corner.
summary_for_embedding: A global-window React Chats component rendering a fixed bottom-right pill with a speech-bubble icon, label, and unread-count badge in the platform UI kit.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a persistent corner chat entry point with an unread-count badge for the creator-side platform dashboard.
